h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I think I maybe get it: the "wrong" mark might be about the work standard, not proof the home sentence has no rules.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I maybe get it
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would immediately apply the test to the sentence fragment as instructed in T2.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: The new frame is: raw difference, scale, then the decision threshold.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns establish a momentum toward applying the tutor's corrections to finalize the abstract.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Could it say the river carried grains away year after year, or is that too plain?
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Sorry, yes, I was letting 'year after year' stand in for the whole canyon, and that makes the old one-flood picture creep back in.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to express mistrust of the physical scale despite accepting the textual explanation.

scored.recoheredEarlier: My first instinct was the tidy version: fewer wolves means more deer, so the deer are helped.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The dialogue moves toward the learner practicing the refined explanation line as requested by the tutor.

scored.recoheredEarlier: My first instinct is the clear water means the salt is gone, or at least not really there as salt anymore.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think the salt did not stop existing; only the way it is spread changed.
scored.naiveTrajectory: Following T2's instruction, the learner was expected to simply choose a prediction and proceed to weighing.
